label: General small molecules
reason: Common starting point for routine reversed-phase small-molecule HPLC methods.

label: Routine QC
reason: 4.6 mm ID, 150/250 mm length and 5 µm particles are common starting points for robust routine HPLC methods.

label: Basic drugs / amines
reason: Endcapped or base-deactivated reversed-phase columns are common starting points for basic analytes where peak tailing is a concern.

label: Acidic compounds
reason: Reversed-phase C18/C8 or phenyl phases are common screening choices for acidic small molecules.

label: Polar compounds
reason: HILIC, amide, amino, cyano, diol and AQ-C18 phases are common starting points for polar compound retention.

label: 100% aqueous methods
reason: AQ-C18 style columns are common starting points when high-aqueous mobile phases are needed.

label: Sugars / carbohydrates
reason: Sugar, amino, amide and ligand-exchange style columns are common screening choices for carbohydrate methods.

label: Amino acids
reason: Amino, HILIC/amide and reversed-phase methods are common starting points depending on derivatization and mobile phase.

label: Peptides
reason: Wide-pore reversed-phase or HILIC/amide columns are common starting points for peptide methods.

label: Proteins / biomolecules
reason: Wide-pore reversed-phase, SEC, HIC and Protein A phases are common screening choices for protein and biomolecule workflows.

label: mAbs / antibodies
reason: SEC, HIC, Protein A and wide-pore reversed-phase columns are common starting points for antibody characterization workflows.

label: Aromatics / PAHs
reason: Phenyl, biphenyl, PFP and C18 phases are common starting points for aromatic and PAH selectivity screening.

label: Halogenated / isomers
reason: PFP, biphenyl and phenyl phases are common screening choices when aromatic, halogenated or isomer selectivity is needed.

label: Ion exchange
reason: SAX, SCX, WAX and WCX phases are common starting points for ion-exchange separation of charged analytes.

label: Size exclusion
reason: SEC/GPC/GFC phases are common starting points when separation by hydrodynamic size or molecular weight is needed.

label: Normal phase
reason: Silica, cyano, amino and diol phases are common starting points for normal-phase style methods.

label: LC-MS
reason: Narrow-bore columns with smaller particles are common starting points for LC-MS and low-flow methods.

label: Fast UHPLC
reason: Small-particle and core-shell columns are common starting points for faster, higher-efficiency separations.

label: Prep purification
reason: Preparative and larger-ID columns are common starting points for purification and loading workflows.

label: Guard / protection
reason: Guard columns are used as a protective front-end to help protect the analytical or preparative column inlet.

label: Vitamins
reason: C18/C8 and AQ-C18/phenyl phases are common screening choices for vitamin methods depending on polarity.

label: Antibiotics
reason: Reversed-phase and polar-retention columns are common screening choices for antibiotic methods depending on structure and ionization.

label: Pesticides / environmental
reason: C18, PFP, phenyl/biphenyl and AQ-C18 phases are common screening choices for pesticide and environmental residue methods.

label: Food testing
reason: Reversed-phase, polar-retention and sugar/amino phases are common screening choices for food and beverage testing methods.
